ip_outcome_note: Card name matches exactly. Final Fantasy Wiki confirms Imperio is the first Divine General in Final Fantasy Dimensions (also titled FF Legends: Warriors of Light and Darkness), who conceals dark alter ego Gehenna. Akira Oguro, original character designer for Dimensions, provided exclusive Opus VIII illustrations for this title. Internal Square Enix IP — no external IP risk.

trivia_bullets.text: Imperio is the first of four Divine Generals serving Emperor Baugauven in Final Fantasy Dimensions (mobile, 2010; localized as Final Fantasy Legends: Warriors of Light and Darkness). He acts as the Emperor's right hand and unofficial leader of the generals — dignified and refined before his true nature is revealed.
sources: TV Tropes: Final Fantasy Dimensions characters

trivia_bullets.text: After his defeat, Imperio's dark alter ego Gehenna — 'the true reflection of Imperio of the Light' — takes over and is fought as a separate boss inside Castle Avalon. Both are destroyed by the Warriors of Light and Darkness. His name is Spanish for 'empire,' a direct thematic marker of his allegiance.

Opus VIII commissioned exclusive original artwork from Akira Oguro for Final Fantasy Legends (Dimensions) cards in the set. Oguro is the original character designer for the Dimensions cast, making this a rare case of the character's creator illustrating them for the TCG.
sources: Square Enix Opus VIII release page (WebSearch snippet)

trivia_bullets.text: Hero rarity (H) in FFTCG sits between Rare and Legendary, with limited print quantities per box. Hero-designating Imperio — Dimensions' primary arch-antagonist — signals Square Enix treated him as a marquee card for the Legends portion of Opus VIII.

related_cards.name: Shango
related_cards.note: Fellow Divine General, explicitly violent — contrasted with Imperio's refinement in canon

related_cards.name: Gehenna
related_cards.note: Imperio's dark alter ego; may be a separate card in the FFTCG corpus
